description_long: This guard rail consists of a long, horizontal metal structure supported by evenly spaced vertical posts. The main rail is composed of a series of parallel bars, with a top and bottom rail connected by vertical and horizontal crossbars, forming a grid-like pattern. The posts are slightly thicker and feature a contrasting color at their tops, possibly for visibility or decorative purposes. The rail appears to be constructed from metal, likely steel, with a dark finish and some reddish-brown accents on the posts. The ends of the rail are blunt and squared off, with no visible flaring or tapering. This type of guard rail is commonly used along roadsides, sidewalks, or in parks to provide a physical barrier for safety and guidance.
description: A long, dark metal guard rail with grid-like crossbars and evenly spaced posts featuring reddish-brown tops. The ends are blunt and the structure is sturdy, suitable for urban safety barriers.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the guard rail, grid pattern, and all vertical posts with colored tops.
description_view_1: Side view showing the narrow profile of the rail and the thickness of the posts.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the grid structure and post arrangement from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and post thickness.
